title: Labcorp (LH) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Labcorp Holdings Inc (LH).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 5% growth rate reflects steady underlying demand for diagnostic testing driven by an aging population, augmented by Labcorp's ongoing strategy of acquiring hospital outreach laboratories, offset by pricing headwinds.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate is applied. While the business is defensive and cash-generative, the higher rate reflects the persistent regulatory risk associated with Medicare reimbursement cuts and dependency on commercial payer contracts.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal rate aligns with long-term healthcare spending growth expectations, acknowledging that diagnostic volume will perpetually scale with demographic trends and population growth.

thesisTitle: Scale and Stability Amidst Pricing Pressure
thesisParagraphs: Labcorp operates within an effective U.S. duopoly in the independent clinical lab space. Its massive national infrastructure—comprising thousands of patient service centers and high-throughput automated testing facilities—creates a nearly insurmountable barrier to entry for new competitors. This scale allows the company to process routine tests at a fraction of the cost of hospital-run laboratories, making it an essential partner in the healthcare ecosystem and driving nearly $1 billion in annual free cash flow.

However, the company remains trapped between strong demographic volume tailwinds and structural pricing headwinds. Medicare reimbursement cuts under PAMA and aggressive negotiation by massive commercial health insurers ensure that Labcorp possesses almost zero pricing power. While currently trading slightly above our calculated fair value, Labcorp remains a solid defensive holding. Future outperformance relies on successfully managing labor costs and continuing to capture market share through hospital lab acquisitions.
verdictFooter: Based on latest available financial data (March 2026).

faq.question: What is driving Labcorp's growth if prices are being cut?
faq.answer: Growth is driven by volume. An aging population requires more testing. Additionally, Labcorp actively acquires the laboratory businesses of hospitals that can no longer afford to run them efficiently.

faq.question: What was the impact of spinning off Fortrea?
faq.answer: Spinning off its clinical development business (Fortrea) allowed Labcorp to become a pure-play diagnostics company. This simplifies the business model and allows management to focus entirely on its core competency.

faq.question: What is PAMA and why does it matter?
faq.answer: The Protecting Access to Medicare Act (PAMA) tied Medicare lab reimbursement rates to private market rates. Because large labs offer steep discounts to private insurers, this legislation has resulted in years of consistent price cuts for Labcorp.
